rulings.Case Title: RE SOCORRO S. ARTATES -TRUST 1992
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent John Artates? Renewed Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Response
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the Court is Respondent John Artates' Motion for Leave to Amend Response. Respondent John Artates previously filed a Motion for Leave to Amend Response on October 5, 2022. At that time, the Motion was assigned a November 9, 2022 hearing date in the Probate Department. On October 11, 2022, after hearing, this court issued Order Setting Trial in the Department of the Presiding Judge, setting trial for October 24, 2022. On November 9, 2022, hearing on the Motion was continued to November 14, 2022 in Department 210, before Judge Vedica Puri. On January 9, 2023, hearing on the Motion was continued to April 10, 2023 in Department 206 for trial call and reassignment. On April 10, 2023, the Motion was taken off calendar, and Respondent was directed to re-notice the Motion with Department 204. Also on April 10, 2023, the matter was removed from the Master Court Calendar, and a Case Management Conference in Department 206 was scheduled for June 13, 2023. The instant Motion was filed on April 18, 2023. There is currently no trial date set for this matter. Having reviewed the moving papers and the opposition, Respondent John Artates' Motion for Leave to Amend Response is GRANTED. Leave to amend a pleading, including an answer or responsive pleading, is entrusted to the sound discretion of the trial court. (See Garcia v. Roberts (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 900, 909; Code Civ. Proc., section 473, subd. (a)(1) [court has discretion "upon any terms as may be just" to allow an amendment to any pleading]; see also Code Civ. Proc., section 576.) "[A]bsent a showing of prejudice to the adverse party, the rule of great liberality in allowing amendment of pleadings will prevail." (Board of Trustees of Leland Stanford Jr. University v. Superior Court (2007) 149 Cal.App.4th 1154, 1163.) Petitioner has failed to show how granting the motion to amend will prejudice petitioner. Respondent John Artates shall submit his amended response (Exhibit 1 to the Declaration of Sony B. Barari filed April 18, 2023) for e-filing within 5 court days after the order giving leave to amend is entered in the docket. Respondent John Artates shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CATHRERINE ANNE PHILLIPS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/31/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Distribution cannot be made to an "estate". Has Warren James Phillips Jr. been APPOINTED by a court as the personal representative of the estate of Warren James Phillips? A certified copy of his Letters must be filed. [LRSF 14.35 G.] (2) What happened to the 2008 Ford Escape Hybrid appraised at $3,500? (3) The transactions involving the sale of the vehicles were not included in the accounting, as alleged in paragraph 16f of the petition. (4) Ending balances shown in the account must be reconciled to the statements or letters. [LRSF 14.34 D.] The accounting shows $266,094.60 on hand as of 12/16/22, but the statement shows $268,080.00 as of 12/14/22 and $266,603.76 as of 1/13/23. (5) Filing fees must be itemized: the filing fee for the ex parte petition was $60 and for this petition was $435, and e-filing costs should be no more than $5 per filing. (6) For what years were IRS federal taxes paid (two times) on 4/15/21? (7) For which years was Michael Senadeno paid for tax preparation on 6/16/21 and 12/8/22? (8) NB: Refunds for amounts paid during estate administration are not receipts (income) includable in the fee base. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/19/23. Only one copy of each paper is required to be provided. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (After all procedural issues are addressed, an appearance will be required for the compensation requested for extraordinary services. The absence of objections does not determine the reasonableness of fees, and the administrator hired and paid his own daughter without prior Court approval. It is unclear why a second supplement and two separate amendments were filed, when all of the issues could/should have been addressed in one amendment to the petition. And there is no need to narrate how issues will be addressed; papers should just be filed, and a fiduciary should just verify his pleadings instead of stating that papers will be filed or that the fiduciary will verify his pleadings. Finally, "its'" is not a word.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HAROLD LEVINE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/17/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Why and when was the trust account for the Survivor's Trust closed when this estate was still open? (2) Please file a copy of the trust that creates the Survivor's Trust to show how the Survivor's Trust was to be distributed. (3) Original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (4) Refunds of amounts paid during estate administration are not receipts (income) includable in the fee base. (5) For which year(s) was $80,145.46 federal tax paid on 12/3/21 and for which years was the CPA paid on 7/12/22 and 10/3/22 for tax preparation? (6) E-filing costs should be no more than $5 per filing, not $6.13 Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/6/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The executor of the will was granted full IAEA authority on 3/24/21. She did not claim her Letters until 4/8/21.) NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DANIEL MARK POMMON JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/31/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Janis Rachel Pommon Pozzi's Petition for Probate a. An amendment was filed on 4/17/23. It was not properly verified by the petitioner. b. An amendment requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] No proof of service of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate, as amended, has been filed. No proof of publication of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate, as amended, has been filed. (Attaching a pleading form of proof of service to a pleading, as in Civil, is NOT appropriate in Probate.) (2) Jonathan Campbell's Petition for Probate of Lost Will a. Still no proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] b. The register of actions amendments filed on 4/12/22 and 3/22/23. No courtesy copies have been received yet by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] c. No proof of service appears to have been filed for either amendment. [CRC 7.53(a).] d. No proof of publication appears to have been filed for either amendment. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/19/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MYRTLE KING REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED SEPTEMBER 2, 2021
rulings.Calendar Matter: Loma J. Hobsons'S Petition To Contest The Validity Of The Myrtle King Revocable Living Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Petitioner has not cleared the procedural issues that were posted on 2/8/23: 1. Notice of Hearing (JC Form DE-120) and a copy of Petition must be given to all persons who are entitled to in the manner Prob. Code 17203. Petitioner filed only Proof of Service By First Class Mail; however JC Form 120 must be used. Also, all heirs at law much be noticed in the manner provided in CCP 413.10 et seq. as Petitioner requests the trust assets to be distributed pursuant to intestate law. 2. Petition must suggest a proposed fiduciary with his/her written consent to act. 3. Proposed Order must be submitted prior to hearing. No responsive document has been filed by Respondent. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/19/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 14.7 C.] Un-endorsed filed copies will be recycled. (2) An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] (3) If the copy of the unfiled amended petition is, in fact, the pleading that was filed, the caption was incomplete, and item 9 was not completed. (4) Item 8 of the amended petition is also incomplete. a. The decedent's brother is presumably an adult. b. What was Ying Shui Seto's relationship to the decedent. If kin, who is/are the successor(s)-in-interest? (They must be identified and must receive notice.) c. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Who is or was the parent of the niblings who is/are or was/were the decedent's sibling(s)? (5) Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. The amended petition was not e-filed until 4/21/23, according to the register of actions, when it was first available to be viewed on the Court's website. Notice mailed on 4/19/23 was defective. (6) If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code Sec. 8113.] (7) Item 2 of the Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities was incomplete (no telephone number). (8) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/10/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHARLOTTE WILLIAMS HAIGHT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/5/2023. This petition appears premature for examiner's review as the following documents are not on file: Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (PC 8100 and 8110); Proposed Order for Probate (LRSF 14.7.A); and Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (LRSF 14.18.E). All required documents must be filed two weeks prior.
